mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in RSAFS has a zero or negative rank IC with subsequent 20-day SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above a threshold signals labor market weakening, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: After the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real interest rates, derived from FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation, raises discount rates and lowers equity present values, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between the change in real interest rates and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p < 0.05) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%), Fed funds rate increases are contractionary and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect flips to positive in low inflation regimes.
failure_condition: In the high inflation regime, positive FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive average 20-day forward SPX returns, or the inverse relationship fails a one-tailed t-test at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals a manufacturing contraction, leading to lower aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After the trigger, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in most cases or the mean return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which boosts GDP growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in HOUST has a rank IC below 0.03 with 5-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarterly GDP growth reading signals the onset of a recession, which sharply reduces corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After a negative GDP growth release, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of cases or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (UNRATE < 4%), strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) fuel wage inflation pressures, prompting Fed tightening and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect reverses in slack labor markets.
failure_condition: In the tight labor market regime, positive PAYEMS changes are associated with positive average 20-day forward SPX returns, or the negative relationship is not statistically significant at the 5% level.
